title: Startup advice — Antonio García Martínez two-traits + monomaniacal focus
html:
Startup advice — Antonio García Martínez two-traits + monomaniacal focus

Source: 2023-07-05 Apple Note. Yahir distills Antonio García Martínez's "How to Run a Startup" into 11 hard-won imperatives — the most concentrated startup-philosophy note in the corpus prior to Rankett.

TL;DR

  • AGM's two-trait founder filter: (1) ability to monomaniacally and obsessively focus on one thing, at the expense of everything else in life, and (2) ability to take and endure endless amounts of shit.
  • "Marketing is like sex — only losers pay for it" — quoted verbatim, becomes a recurring AGM-ism in Yahir's frame.
  • "Have a leader: ONE captain making the calls" — single-point-of-decision principle.
  • "Before meeting someone, know every goddamn thing about the person. Or else you are failing."
  • "If involved in something of secrecy, phone calls are the way to prove if something is juicy. If emails are the main form of communication, then nothing is going on."
  • "Larger companies have much more to fear of you than you have of them" — counterweight to the Goliath complex.
  • "Whenever you face a stressful risky challenge, firewall the rest of the company from the mess" — proto-CEO containment principle.

Pattern observed

This is a founder-mindset transmission file. Two months before the Egypt trip and well before Brotein and Rankett existed, Yahir was already metabolizing AGM's framework. The "monomaniacal focus" line shows up in the 2024 future thoughts note when he says he must "lock in for the next chapter."

Open questions

  • Was this from AGM's Chaos Monkeys book or a podcast?
  • Did Yahir ever print the "How to Run a Startup" essay and give it to Cam? (He says he plans to.)

Related

  • antonio garcia martinez
  • rankett
  • brotein
  • no runway discipline
